questions.question: Write a letter to your mother to wish her happy birthday and express your love and gratitude on this special occasion.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Mother,

I am writing this letter to wish you a very happy birthday. This day is truly special for me because it celebrates the most wonderful person in my life.

I want you to know how grateful I am for everything you do for our family. I still remember how, during the Covid lockdown, you taught yourself to make the most amazing sandwiches, pasta and paneer tikka. Those dishes have now become my absolute favourites, and I look forward to them every week. You always make sure I eat well before my mridangam practice and after school, and I never take that for granted.

I also cherish the effort you put into my birthday celebrations every year. The chenda cake you arranged in 2023 was incredible, and I was so thrilled when you and Vandana Aunty surprised me with the chocolate cake decorated with a tabla and tambura theme. Those memories are very close to my heart.

Mother, you are my biggest supporter. Whether it is my studies, my music or my cricket, you are always there cheering me on. I promise that I will buy you a beautiful gift from my very first earning. Until then, please accept all my love and hugs.

Wishing you the happiest birthday ever.

Your loving son,
Anirudh

questions.question: Some students recently participated in interschool competitions and missed classes during training and on competition days. Request your Principal to organise some extra classes for those students.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Principal,
Sishya School,
Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

Dear Madam,

Subject: Request for extra classes for students who missed lessons during interschool competitions

I am writing this letter to bring to your kind attention that a large number of students from our class missed several important lessons while participating in the recent interschool music competition.

The practice sessions for the competition took an entire week, during which we could not attend regular classes. As a result, we missed the Matter chapter in Physics, which is part of the syllabus for the upcoming first round test. The majority of our class participated in this music competition, so a significant number of students are affected.

The notes for the chapter are quite elaborate and it would be very tedious and stressful to try and catch up on our own, especially with the test approaching soon. Without proper teaching, we fear our performance in the round test will be badly affected.

I kindly request you to arrange a few extra classes after school hours so that we can cover the missed portions properly. This would greatly help us prepare for the upcoming test and ease our anxiety.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: [F.1] Write an informal letter to your mother on her birthday to express your love and gratitude on this special occasion.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Mother,

I am writing this letter to wish you the happiest birthday. Today is a day I always look forward to because it is all about celebrating you.

Thank you for being the most caring and loving mother anyone could ask for. You wake up early every single day to make sure I have a good breakfast before school. You sit with me patiently when I struggle with my homework, and you never lose your temper even when I ask the same doubt three times. The way you prepare my favourite paneer tikka and pasta on weekends makes me feel so loved.

I especially want to thank you for always encouraging me in my music. Whether it is driving me to my mridangam class or listening to me practise at home, you have always supported my passion. I also love the effort you put into my birthday cakes every year. The themed cakes with musical instruments were the best surprise I have ever received.

Mother, you are the heart of our family. I hope this birthday brings you all the happiness and peace you deserve. I will work hard in my studies and music to make you proud.

Wishing you a wonderful year ahead.

Your loving son,
Anirudh

questions.question: [F.2] Your brother has been falling ill very frequently. Advise him to engage in outdoor activities and wean himself off watching too much television or playing computer games.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Prahlad,

I am writing this letter because I have been quite worried about your health lately. Mothermentioned that you have been falling ill very frequently, and I think I know what might be causing it.

I have noticed that whenever I visit you, you spend most of your time watching television or playing computer games. Sitting indoors for long hours is not good for your body or your eyes. When we do not move around enough, our immunity becomes weak, and that is why you keep catching fevers and colds.

I would strongly advise you to start playing outdoor games. You can join a cricket coaching class like I did, or even start with simple activities like cycling or going for evening walks in your neighbourhood park. When I play cricket with my friends, I feel so energetic and refreshed afterwards. Outdoor activities not only keep us physically fit but also improve our mood and help us sleep better at night.

Try to limit your screen time to one hour a day. Instead, you could also pick up a sport like badminton or football. Trust me, once you start enjoying the outdoors, you will not miss the screen at all.

Please take care of yourself. I look forward to playing cricket with you soon.

Your loving brother,
Anirudh

questions.question: [F.3] A friend recently lost her pet and is in grief. Express empathy for her loss and offer your condolences.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Suvasana,

I am writing this letter because I heard about the loss of your beloved pet, and I wanted you to know how deeply sorry I am.

I understand how much your pet meant to you. I have a dog named Nandi, and he is like a member of our family. I cannot even imagine how painful it must be to lose a companion who was always there to greet you when you came home. Pets give us unconditional love, and losing them leaves a void that is very hard to fill.

I remember how happy you always were when you talked about your pet at school. The little stories you shared about your pet's funny habits always made us laugh. Those are beautiful memories, and I hope they bring you comfort during this difficult time.

Please do not feel that you have to be strong all the time. It is perfectly normal to grieve, and it shows how much love you had for your pet. If you ever want to talk or if you need company, I am always here for you. Maybe this weekend, you could come over and spend some time with Nandi. I am sure he would love to meet you, and it might help lift your spirits.

Take care of yourself, and know that I am thinking of you.

Your loving friend,
Anirudh

questions.question: [F.4] Share your birthday plans with your friends, and inform them of the date, time and venue, so that they can join in.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Yohann,

I am writing this letter to share some exciting news with you. My birthday is coming up soon, and I have planned a wonderful celebration. I would love for you to be there.

The party is on Saturday, the 18th of April, 2026, starting at 4:00 PM. It will be held at our house on 22nd Cross Street, Indira Nagar, Adyar. I have planned a fun evening with lots of games, music and of course, plenty of delicious food. Motheris preparing some of her special dishes, and we have also ordered a really cool cake this year.

I have also arranged for us to play cricket in the evening in the open area near our house. After that, we will have dinner together. I know how much you enjoy cricket, so I thought it would be the perfect way to celebrate. I have invited Suvasana and a few other friends from school as well.

Please make sure you keep the evening free. It would mean a lot to me if you could come. Let me know if you need directions to reach my house, and I will send you the location on my father's phone.

I am really looking forward to celebrating with you.

Your loving friend,
Anirudh

questions.question: [F.5] You just returned from a visit to your aunt and uncle. Thank them for their hospitality and for the unique experience of engaging in water sports with them.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Aunt and Uncle,

I am writing this letter to thank you both from the bottom of my heart for the amazing time I had during my visit to your place last week. It was truly one of the best holidays I have ever had.

Your hospitality was incredible. From the moment I arrived, you made me feel completely at home. The food was absolutely delicious, and I especially loved the special breakfast you prepared every morning. I felt so pampered and cared for throughout my stay.

The highlight of the trip, without a doubt, was the water sports experience. I had never tried kayaking before, and it was thrilling to paddle through the water with Uncle guiding me. The jet skiing was an absolute adventure, and I could not stop smiling the entire time. I was a little nervous about trying parasailing at first, but with your encouragement, I gathered the courage, and it turned out to be the most exhilarating experience of my life. Seeing the coastline from up above was breathtaking.

Thank you for creating such wonderful memories for me. I have been telling all my friends at Sishya about the trip, and they are quite jealous. I hope you will visit us in Chennai soon so that I can show you around Marina Beach.

With lots of love and gratitude.

Your loving nephew,
Anirudh

questions.question: [F.6] You just found out the address of your childhood friend. Express your joy and share some cherished memories of childhood with her/him.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

My dear Vishwak,

I am writing this letter because I just found out your new address, and I could not contain my excitement. It has been such a long time since we last spoke, and I have missed you terribly.

Do you remember the summers we used to spend together at Thatha's house? We would wake up early and run straight to the garden to play cricket with a tennis ball. You were always the bowler, and I was always the batsman, and we would argue endlessly about the rules. Those were some of the happiest days of my childhood.

I also fondly remember how we used to sit together and watch cartoons for hours until our grandmothers would scold us and send us outside to play. And the time we tried to build a fort out of old bedsheets and pillows, only for it to collapse on us within minutes. We laughed so hard that day.

So much has changed since then. I am now studying in Class 8C at Sishya School in Adyar, Chennai. I have been learning the mridangam and play cricket regularly with my school friends. I would love to know what you have been up to.

Please write back to me soon. I hope we can meet during the summer holidays and relive some of those wonderful childhood memories.

Your loving friend,
Anirudh

questions.question: [G.1] Some students recently participated in interschool competitions and missed classes during training and on competition days. Request your Principal to organise some extra classes for those students.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Principal,
Sishya School,
Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

Dear Madam,

Subject: Request for extra classes for students who missed lessons during interschool competitions

I am writing this letter to respectfully request you to arrange extra classes for the students who missed regular lessons while participating in the recent interschool competitions.

A large group of students from Class 8C represented our school in the interschool music and sports competitions held last month. The rehearsals and practice sessions began a week before the events, during which we were unable to attend our regular classes. On the competition days as well, we had to be at the venue the entire day and missed all our lessons.

As a result, we have fallen behind in several subjects, especially in Physics and Mathematics where new chapters were introduced during our absence. With the periodic tests approaching in a few weeks, we are very concerned about our preparation. Copying notes from classmates is helpful, but it cannot replace proper teaching and explanation from our teachers.

I kindly request you to consider organising a few extra classes after school hours for the affected students. Even two or three sessions per subject would help us catch up with the rest of the class. We are willing to stay back after school on any days that are convenient for the teachers.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: [G.2] Blaring loudspeakers in your area have disturbed the studies and sleep of the residents. Draw the attention of the Police Commissioner and seek action.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Commissioner of Police,
Chennai City Police,
Vepery,
Chennai - 600007.

Dear Sir,

Subject: Complaint regarding excessive noise from loudspeakers in Indira Nagar, Adyar

I am writing this letter to draw your kind attention to the severe noise pollution caused by blaring loudspeakers in our residential area of Indira Nagar, Adyar.

For the past two weeks, loudspeakers have been operating at extremely high volumes from a nearby event hall until well past midnight. The noise is so loud that it can be heard clearly inside our homes even with all the windows and doors shut. This has been happening almost every other day, and the situation is becoming unbearable.

The excessive noise has severely affected the daily lives of all the residents. Students like myself are unable to concentrate on our studies, especially during the evening hours when we do our homework and prepare for tests. Elderly residents and young children are unable to sleep at night. Several families have complained about headaches and stress caused by the constant disturbance.

As per the Noise Pollution Rules, loudspeakers should not be used beyond 10:00 PM and their volume levels must be within permissible limits. I request you to kindly take strict action against the violators and ensure that the rules are enforced in our area. The well-being and peace of the entire neighbourhood is at stake.

Awaiting a positive reply and prompt action.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: [G.3] You recently forgot your credit card with your bank manager. Request him/her to dispatch it to your residential address.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Branch Manager,
State Bank of India,
Adyar Branch,
Chennai - 600020.

Dear Sir,

Subject: Request to dispatch credit card left at the branch

I am writing this letter to inform you that I accidentally left my credit card at your branch during my visit on 28th March, 2026, and to request you to kindly dispatch it to my residential address.

I had visited the Adyar branch on the above-mentioned date to update my account details. During the process, I handed over my credit card to the teller at Counter 3 for verification purposes. After completing the formalities, I inadvertently left the branch without collecting my card. I realised my mistake only after reaching home later that evening.

The details of the credit card are as follows: it is a State Bank of India Visa credit card, and the name on the card is Ram Sabode. I contacted the branch over the phone the next day, and your staff confirmed that the card has been safely kept at the branch.

As I am currently unable to visit the branch in person due to my work commitments, I kindly request you to dispatch the credit card to my residential address mentioned above through registered post or a secure courier service. I shall be grateful for your prompt assistance in this matter.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Ram Sabode

questions.question: [G.4] The quality of vegetables and fruits is poor at the cooperative stores in your area, and the prices are unfair. Bring this to the notice of the Zonal Manager of the cooperative stores.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Zonal Manager,
Tamil Nadu Cooperative Stores,
Adyar Zone,
Chennai - 600020.

Dear Sir,

Subject: Complaint regarding poor quality and unfair pricing of vegetables and fruits

I am writing this letter to bring to your notice the poor quality of vegetables and fruits being sold at the cooperative store in Indira Nagar, Adyar, along with the unreasonably high prices being charged.

The vegetables available at the store are often wilted, stale and sometimes even rotten. On multiple occasions, my family has purchased tomatoes and brinjals that were spoiled on the inside despite looking acceptable from the outside. The fruits are no better. The bananas are often overripe, and the apples have bruise marks all over them. It is clear that the store is not maintaining proper cold storage or following any quality checks before putting the produce on display.

Moreover, the prices being charged are significantly higher than the rates at other nearby markets. For example, onions are being sold at nearly double the market rate, and the price of potatoes is at least thirty percent higher than what other vendors charge. This is very unfair to the residents who depend on the cooperative store for their daily needs, trusting it to offer reasonable prices.

I request you to kindly look into this matter urgently. Please ensure that fresh produce is supplied to the store and that prices are kept fair and in line with market rates.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: [G.5] Draw attention of the Archaeological Society of India to the poor condition of a historical monument in your city.
questions.answer:
16/8, 22nd Cross Street,
Indira Nagar, Adyar,
Chennai - 600020.

2nd April, 2026

The Director General,
Archaeological Survey of India,
Chennai Circle,
Chennai - 600009.

Dear Sir,

Subject: Poor condition of the San Thome Basilica and surrounding heritage structures in Chennai

I am writing this letter to draw your attention to the deteriorating condition of the historic San Thome Basilica and the heritage structures in its vicinity in Mylapore, Chennai.

During a recent school visit, I was deeply saddened to see the state of this important monument. The outer walls of the basilica have developed cracks in several places, and the paint is peeling off badly. The beautiful stained glass windows, which are a major attraction, are covered in dust and some of them appear to be damaged. The surrounding garden area is poorly maintained, with overgrown weeds and broken benches scattered around.

Moreover, visitors have been scribbling graffiti on the walls of the adjacent heritage structures. There are no proper signboards or barriers to prevent people from touching or damaging the ancient surfaces. The lack of security and maintenance staff is very evident. Litter and plastic waste can be seen lying around the premises, which is very disrespectful to a monument of such historical importance.

I request you to kindly take immediate steps to restore and maintain this monument. Deploying security guards, installing CCTV cameras and carrying out regular cleaning and repair work would go a long way in preserving this valuable piece of our heritage.

Awaiting a positive reply.

Thanking you,
Yours truly,
Anirudh Ramkumar

questions.question: [H.1] Your family has planned to visit Goa during New Year, so you need to book a room with a sea view from the balcony. Write an email to the manager of a hotel to book a room for your family.
questions.answer:
From: ram.sabode@gmail.com
To: reservations@grandgoahotel.com
Subject: Room reservation with sea view for New Year holiday

Dear Sir/Madam,

I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to enquire about room availability and to make a reservation at your hotel for the New Year holiday period.

My family of four is planning to visit Goa from 28th December, 2026 to 2nd January, 2027, and we would like to book a spacious room with a sea view from the balcony. We would prefer a room on a higher floor so that we can enjoy an unobstructed view of the ocean.

Kindly let us know the following details:
1. Availability of a sea-view room with a balcony for the above dates.
2. The tariff per night, including applicable taxes.
3. Whether breakfast is included in the room charges.
4. Any special New Year packages or offers you may have.

We would also appreciate it if the room has an extra bed or a pull-out sofa, as we have two children. If you could also share information about nearby restaurants and water sports activities, it would be very helpful for our trip planning.

Please confirm the booking at your earliest convenience so that we can finalise our travel arrangements accordingly.

Looking forward to your reply.

Yours sincerely,
Ram Sabode
Contact: +91-98XXX-XXXXX

questions.question: [H.2] The oldest and most favourite teacher at your school, Ms Archana, is going to retire soon. As the House Captain, write an email to the administrator to help you book an auditorium in the nearby locality for the farewell ceremony.
questions.answer:
From: anirudh.ramkumar@sishya.edu.in
To: admin@sishya.edu.in
Subject: Request to book an auditorium for Ms Archana's farewell ceremony

Dear Sir/Madam,

I hope this email finds you well. I am Anirudh Ramkumar, the House Captain of Class 8C. I am writing to request your assistance in booking an auditorium in the nearby locality for the farewell ceremony of our beloved teacher, Ms Archana, who is retiring at the end of this academic year.

Ms Archana has been an integral part of Sishya School for over three decades, and she holds a very special place in the hearts of all the students and staff. We would like to organise a grand farewell ceremony to honour her years of dedicated service and to express our heartfelt gratitude.

We are planning the event for the last Saturday of the term, and we expect around 200 attendees, including current students, alumni, parents and fellow teachers. We would need an auditorium that can accommodate this number comfortably and has a stage with a sound system for speeches and cultural performances.

Could you kindly help us with the following:
1. Checking the availability of auditoriums near Adyar, such as the Bharatiya Vidya Bhavan hall or the Arkay Convention Centre.
2. Comparing the rental charges for a four-hour booking.
3. Confirming whether the venue provides basic audio-visual equipment.

We would be grateful if this could be finalised within the next two weeks so that we can proceed with the rest of the arrangements.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Yours sincerely,
Anirudh Ramkumar
House Captain, Class 8C
Sishya School, Adyar

questions.question: [H.3] You have recently come across an advertisement for a summer internship at the neighbourhood library. Write an application email to the head librarian applying for this internship.
questions.answer:
From: anirudh.ramkumar@sishya.edu.in
To: headlibrarian@adyarlibrary.org
Subject: Application for summer internship at Adyar Library

Dear Madam,

I hope this email finds you well. I am Anirudh Ramkumar, a Class 8 student at Sishya School, Adyar. I recently came across the advertisement for a summer internship at your library, and I am writing to express my keen interest in applying for the same.

I have always been an avid reader and a regular visitor to our school library. I enjoy reading books across a wide range of subjects, from science and history to adventure stories and biographies. The idea of spending my summer holidays learning how a library functions and helping others discover the joy of reading greatly excites me.

I believe I would be a suitable candidate for this internship for the following reasons:
1. I am well-organised and responsible. As the class monitor, I have experience managing classroom resources and keeping records.
2. I am comfortable using computers and can help with cataloguing or digital inventory if required.
3. I have good communication skills and would be happy to assist visitors in locating books and resources.
4. I am punctual, hardworking and eager to learn new things.

The internship would be a wonderful learning experience for me, and I assure you that I will carry out all assigned duties with sincerity and dedication. I am available for the entire duration of the summer holidays, from May to June 2026.

Looking forward to a favourable reply.

Yours sincerely,
Anirudh Ramkumar
Class 8C, Sishya School, Adyar
Contact: +91-98XXX-XXXXX
